heading: Office of Travel Promotion
label: 2123 Office of Travel Promotion

children.heading: Office established
children.label: a Office established
children.text: There is established within the Department of Commerce an office to be known as the Office of Travel Promotion.

children.heading: Appointment
children.label: 1 Appointment
children.text: The Office shall be headed by a Director who shall be appointed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Qualifications
children.label: 2 Qualifications
children.text: The Director shall be a citizen of the United States and have experience in a field directly related to the promotion of travel to and within the United States.

children.heading: Duties
children.label: 3 Duties
children.text: The Director shall be responsible for ensuring the office is carrying out its functions effectively and shall report to the Secretary.

serve as liaison to the Corporation for Travel Promotion established by subsection (b) of section 11 of the Travel Promotion Act of 2009 and support and encourage the development of programs to increase the number of international visitors to the United States for business, leisure, educational, medical, exchange, and other purposes;1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: A
children.text: to disseminate information more effectively to potential international visitors about documentation and procedures required for admission to the United States as a visitor;

children.label: B
children.text: to ensure that arriving international visitors are generally welcomed with accurate information and in an inviting manner;

children.label: C
children.text: to collect accurate data on the total number of international visitors that visit each State; and

children.label: D
children.text: enhance the entry and departure experience for international visitors through the use of advertising, signage, and customer service; and
support State, regional, and private sector initiatives to promote travel to and within the United States.

children.heading: Reports to Congress
children.label: d Reports to Congress
children.text: Within a year after , and periodically thereafter as appropriate, the Secretary shall transmit a report to the Senate Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation, the Senate Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs, the Senate Committee on Foreign Relations, the House of Representatives Committee on Energy and Commerce, the House of Representatives Committee on Homeland Security, and the House of Representatives Committee on Foreign Affairs describing the Office’s work with the Corporation, the Secretary of State and the Secretary of Homeland Security to carry out subsection (c)(2).March 4, 2010
